records.ety: [OE. varien, F. varier, L. variare, fr. varius various. See Various, and cf. Variate.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Varied (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Varying.]

defs: To change the aspect of; to alter in form, appearance, substance, position, or the like; to make different by a partial change; to modify; as, to vary the properties, proportions, or nature of a thing; to vary a posture or an attitude; to vary one's dress or opinions.
quotes: Shall we vary our device at will, Even as new occasion appears?

defs: To change to something else; to transmute; to exchange; to alternate.
quotes: Gods, that never change their state, Vary oft their love and hate.
We are to vary the customs according to the time and country where the scene of action lies.

defs: To make of different kinds; to make different from one another; to diversify; to variegate.
quotes: God hath here Varied his bounty so with new delights.

defs: To embellish; to change fancifully; to present under new aspects, as of form, key, measure, etc. See Variation, 4.
To alter, or be altered, in any manner; to suffer a partial change; to become different; to be modified; as, colors vary in different lights.
quotes: That each from other differs, first confess; Next, that he varies from himself no less.

defs: To differ, or be different; to be unlike or diverse; as, the laws of France vary from those of England.
To alter or change in succession; to alternate; as, one mathematical quantity varies inversely as another.
quotes: While fear and anger, with alternate grace, Pant in her breast, and vary in her face.

defs: To deviate; to depart; to swerve; -- followed by from; as, to vary from the law, or from reason.
To disagree; to be at variance or in dissension; as, men vary in opinion.
quotes: The rich jewel which we vary for.
